query_used: Condition: Severe shoulder injury, suspected rotator cuff tear or ligament damage | Stage: Subacute | Symptoms: Sharp pain during hand movements, throbbing pain during rest, frequent intense tingling sensations in Front and side of the shoulder; extends down to the arm | Onset: About two weeks after lifting a heavy object | Functional Limitations: Raising arm or lifting shoulder is extremely difficult | Red Flags: Large swelling in the area (about 8-9 inches), spreading pain, numbness and tingling in the hand

the management of rotator common reason for a person with shoulder pain to be treated in the office. cuff injuries what are the risk factors for a rotator cuff tear? in general, there is a higher risk for a rotator cuff tear as we age. the most common time for being diagnosed is during the 40s and beyond because of the normal wear and tear to the tendons. in some cases, there is little to no pain caused by these tears. in younger patients, especially those that are part of overhead sports (tennis, baseball pitchers), tears can come from repeat trauma to the tendon from the force needed to play the sport. it is more common for a younger patient to get a tear from a specific injury such as a shoulder dislocation. how are rotator cuff tears diagnosed? there is strong support showing that an background examination by your orthopaedist can help to diagnose whether you have a tear. however, there this plain language summary provides an overview is also strong evidence that the use of imaging of the surgical and non surgical management for studies such as an mri (magnetic resonance rotator cuff tears in adults over 18 years of age. imaging) or an ultrasound (machine using sound the rotator cuff is a group of four tissues that waves), when used with the clinical examination, connect to bones (tendons) and muscles that act can increase the chance for identifying a rotator together to help you lift and rotate your shoulder so cuff tear. that you can perform activities such as reaching for what are the treatment options for a tear? final_llm_response: {"summary": "The patient presents with a rotator cuff tear. The management of rotator cuff tears is complex and depends on the size, location, and severity of the tear. Conservative treatment options include physical therapy, anti-inflammatory medications, and injections. Surgical intervention may be necessary for larger or more severe tears. The decision to undergo surgery should involve a thorough discussion between the patient and their orthopedic surgeon.", "insights": "The management of rotator cuff tears is complex and depends on the size, location, and severity of the tear. Conservative treatment options include physical therapy, anti-inflammatory medications, and injections. Surgical intervention may be necessary for larger or more severe tears. The decision to undergo surgery should involve a thorough discussion between the patient and their orthopedic surgeon.", "treatment_steps": ["Initial consultation", "Imaging studies", "Diagnosis", "Conservative treatment", "Surgical intervention"], "sources_used": ["Rotator Cuff Injuries CPG (AAOS, 2025)", "Comparative clinical evaluation of two techniques of arthroscopic treatment of partial articular rotator cuff tears (Revista Brasileira de Orthopedia, 2021)"], "red_flag": false, "disclaimer": "This is a computer-generated clinical insight, not a replacement for professional medical advice."}
